findings.summary: The acct_wrapper module contains a permission bypass: the 'we_flag' input directly influences the access control output (acc_ctrl_o) without any lock or privilege check, bypassing the register lock mechanism (reglk_ctrl) that protects the acct_mem access control registers.

evidence.description: Input we_flag used to directly influence access control output without protection.
evidence.supports_claim: we_flag is an external input that can modify permission bits.

evidence.description: Register lock control signal used to gate reads/writes to acct_mem.
evidence.supports_claim: Shows the intended protection mechanism that is bypassed.

evidence.description: Access control output uses logical OR with {8{we_flag}}, allowing we_flag to set the low 8 bits of acct_mem[0] in the output without any lock check.
Access control memory holding the permission state.
evidence.supports_claim: Identifies the protected resource whose values are being leaked/overridden.

evidence.description: Output access control values used to enforce downstream permissions.
evidence.supports_claim: The bypass directly impacts this security-critical output.

findings.reasoning_summary: The module uses reglk_ctrl lock bits to prevent unauthorized modification or reading of acct_mem via the AXI interface. However, the output acc_ctrl_o is combinationally derived as {acct_mem[2], acct_mem[1], acct_mem[0] | {8{we_flag}}}. This injects the external we_flag directly into the access control bits, completely bypassing the lock logic. There is no authentication, gating, or privilege check on we_flag.
findings.security_impact: An agent that controls the we_flag signal can set critical access control bits to grant permissions (or disable protections) without needing to unlock the registers, potentially leading to unauthorized access to peripherals or system resources.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The origin and control of we_flag are not visible in the observed code; it may be tied to a debug interface or internal test logic. Nevertheless, the bypass path is clearly present in the RTL.
